preamble: A mysterious appearance of a back vowel where once there was silence. Epenthesis often serves to break up difficult consonant clusters or to provide a landing pad for stress at the beginning or end of words.

examples.note: Inserion of /u/ to break up consonant clusters in specific morphological contexts.
Vowel epenthesis in final position or near labialized consonants.

sources: Watson, J. C. E. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
Kossmann, M. (2013). The Arabic Influence on Northern Berber.
